past_recommendations.text: Log per-signal criterion feature values (pct_from_9ema, prior_day_range_atr, rvol_score, pct_change_3, gap_pct, reversal_pct) into the outcome table so criterion-level threshold analysis is possible next cycle.

Surface episode_signal_num and prior-print outcome in scanner output — a reprint following an already-tradeable first print ran 13/14 and is the strongest entry filter found; reprints generally (58–91%) beat first prints (42%).
Adopt ~1.5-ATR initial stop guidance for reversal entries — covers 27/28 winners' pre-target adverse while losers run median 1.66-ATR MAE; sub-0.5-ATR stops would have knocked out 13/28 winners.

Investigate a regime/breadth gate for Large-cap reversals (e.g., sector-wide squeeze detection) — Large GO ran 7/22 with the Apr-30→May-6 semis melt-up contributing 7 correlated failures at ~3-ATR median MAE.
Track the 4/5-vs-5/5 inversion (16/29 vs 6/16) over the next batch before acting; if it persists at n≥20 per cell, evaluate whether the 5th criterion is selecting unshortable peak momentum.
Document that first-print fixed holds to D+3 have negative expectancy (mean −1.5%) — exits must be MFE-managed (1-ATR target hit in ≤1 day median) rather than time-based.
Defer all bounce-side conclusions until n ≥ 20 independent episodes (currently 7).

reversal | POOLED | prior_day_rvol | score-point only (per-cap 1.25–2.0) | hard floor: suppress/NOGO any signal with prior_day_rvol < 1.25 | 2/21 (10%) tradeable below 1.25 vs 26/46 (57%) above; within Large (n=32): 0/12 below vs 9/20 above; only 2 winners lost (WOLF 1.10, NOK 1.07); cluster-independent (2 of 21 sub-1.25 rows in the May melt-up)

reversal | POOLED | pct_from_9ema | per-cap score-point (0.04–0.80) | secondary hard floor at 0.10 pooled | ≤0.10: 2/16 (12%) vs >0.10: 26/51 (51%); after the RVOL veto it incrementally drops 7 more losers for 2 winners (AMD 0.054, TSLA 0.052, both Large); combined RVOL+9EMA gate: pass 24/37 (65%) vs fail 4/30 (13%) — adopt only if the RVOL veto alone proves insufficient, since within-Large medians barely separate (0.128 vs 0.107)
Implement the pooled prior_day_rvol ≥ 1.25 hard veto on reversal signals (downgrade to NOGO/suppress, not just a lost score point) — strongest single filter found this cycle, 10% vs 57% tradeable.

Re-evaluate gap_pct as a reversal criterion next cycle — zero pooled separation (W med 0.014 vs L med 0.011, flat bins, n=67); if still flat at ~100 first-flags, replace its score point with an RVOL-tier point so the 5-point score stops rewarding noise (also a candidate cause of the 4/5 > 5/5 inversion).

Continue tracking the 4/5-vs-5/5 inversion (now 16/29 vs 6/16 first-flags, second consecutive cycle, 5/5 n=16 < 20) — check next cycle whether sub-1.25-RVOL and gap-credit signals account for the 5/5 underperformance before restructuring the score.

Reprint persistence strengthened to 28/30 (93%) after a tradeable prior print vs 18/40 (45%) otherwise — escalate Analysis #1's recommendation to surface episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status in scanner output from "recommended" to "highest-priority", with the caveat that prior tradeability must already be observable (target printed) at reprint time.

Document that D0-close-direction confirmation was tested and rejected (48% vs 37% lift, costs 14/28 winners incl. all 9 same-day payers) and that long-first tactics are unsupported (~58% long-side 1-ATR hit, adverse to thesis) — stop relitigating entry-delay variants; the edge levers are the RVOL veto, the reprint trigger, and the 1.5-ATR stop (now 27/28 winners survive, 25/39 losers capped).

Large-cap reversal weakness persists ex-cluster (9/28, 32% vs Medium 58%) — after deploying the RVOL veto (which removes all 12 sub-1.25 Large losers), re-measure Large before pursuing the breadth/regime gate from Analysis #1.

Bounce side remains deferred at 7 independent episodes (4/7 tradeable); flag for review that the lone bounce GO (CAR 5/6) was the dataset's worst signal — when n≥20, test whether the bounce score is inverted on mid-crash knife-catches (prior_day_range_atr 4.1 on that signal).
